data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized SDK-style replacement:

## Legacy Patterns Identified

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Solution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + `xmlns` on `<Project>` | Replace with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | All defaults in SDK-style — remove |
| 4 | Separate Debug/Release `PropertyGroup`s | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 5 | Explicit `<Reference>` for BCL assemblies | Implicit for .NET Framework projects |
| 6 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Auto-globbed (`**/*.cs`) in SDK-style |
| 7 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Auto-generated; metadata moves to `<PropertyGroup>` |
| 8 | Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Implicit via SDK |

## Modernized Project File

The entire 47-line `.csproj` reduces to:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style Project element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` attributes
2. **Explicit Microsoft.Common.props import** — SDK-style handles this automatically
3. **Verbose PropertyGroup** with `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` — most are defaults in SDK-style
4. **Separate Debug/Release PropertyGroups** — SDK-style provides sensible defaults for these


5. **Explicit framework assembly references** like System and System.Core that become implicit in SDK-style
6. **Hardcoded Compile items** replaced by automatic glob patterns that include all .cs files
7. **AssemblyInfo.cs file** that SDK-style auto-generates, with metadata moving directly into the project file
8. **Explicit target imports** that the SDK handles automatically
9. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** that translates to `net472` in the new format

Now I'll modernize this project.

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines**. Here's what was removed/simplified:

- **XML declaration, xmlns, ToolsVersion** — unnecessary with SDK-style
- **Props/targets imports** — implicit via `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
- **Debug/Release property groups** — SDK defaults match (optimize, debug symbols, output paths)
- **BCL references** (System, System.Core, etc.) — implicit for `net472`
- **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK auto-globs all `*.cs` files
- **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — replaced by `<Description>`, `<Company>`, `<Copyright>`, `<Version>` in the csproj
- **OutputType** — `Library` is the default, so omitted
